Reusable components shipped

Product stills - launch cycle01 - 04

The Brief

A banking dashboard that earns belief before the first tap.

Company One was entering a crowded market with strong investors, serious product ambition, and a trust problem every fintech faces at launch. The experience had to look established before the brand actually was.

We focused on hierarchy, rhythm, and restraint. Every screen needed to feel exacting without becoming sterile. Dense information became readable through spacing, stronger grouping, and a component system designed around confidence.

The final product shipped with a visual language that felt both immediate and scalable. It helped the product team move faster internally while reassuring users that their money was in careful hands.

What We Built

01P

Product Design

End-to-end flows for onboarding, account management, transfers, and investment touchpoints.

02S

Design System

A disciplined component library built to keep data-heavy screens calm and consistent.

03D

Data Visualisation

Charts and summaries tuned for speed of comprehension under real financial pressure.

04O

Onboarding Flow

Trust-building first-run experience that reduces hesitation and accelerates completion.

05T

Theme Modes

Dark and light presentation tuned for both premium feel and long-session usability.

06A

Accessibility QA

Contrast, keyboard, and assistive-support standards baked in from system level upward.

Product Launch

"The interface needed to feel proven on day one. That was the design brief in one sentence."

How trust became the interface.

01
Discovery
We audited competitor dashboards, interviewed early investors, and studied where users hesitated when money felt risky.
02
Strategy
The product vision centered on clarity under pressure: fewer decisions, stronger hierarchy, and confidence in every flow.
03
Design
We built a polished interface system where charts, balances, and actions could coexist without visual fatigue.
04
Delivery
A complete component library, responsive patterns, and QA standards shipped alongside the launch-ready product.

"

We had tried two other agencies before this. Nobody else understood that trust is a design problem. This team gets it at a level that is genuinely rare.
